content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA of length NNN, we can define rotation as follows. If we rotate AAA to the right, all elements move to the right one unit, and the last element moves to the beginning. That is, it becomes [AN,A1,A2,‚Ä¶,AN‚ąí1][A_N, A_1,A_2,\ldots,A_{N-1}][AN‚Äč,A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚ąí1‚Äč]. Similarly if we rotate AAA to the left, it becomes [A2,A3,‚Ä¶,AN,A1][A_2,A_3,\ldots,A_N, A_1][A2‚Äč,A3‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč,A1‚Äč].

Given an array AAA and an integer xxx, define f(A,x)f(A,x)f(A,x) to be the array AAA rotated by the amount xxx. If x‚Č•0x\ge 0x‚Č•0, this means we rotate it right xxx times. If x<0x<0x<0, this means we rotate it left ‚ą£x‚ą£|x|‚ą£x‚ą£ times.

You are given an array AAA of length NNN. Then QQQ queries follow. In each query, an integer xxx is given. To answer the query, you should replace AAA with A+f(A,x)A+f(A,x)A+f(A,x) where +++ denotes concatenation. After this operation, you must output the sum of all elements of AAA. Since this number can be large, output it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

Note that the queries are cumulative. When you modify AAA to answer one query, it starts that way for the next query.

###Input


The first line contains an integer NNN - the size of the initial array.
The second line contains NNN integers A1,‚Ä¶,ANA_1,\ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč - the elements of the initial array.
The third line contains an integer QQQ - the number of queries.
The fourth line contains QQQ space-separated integers x1,‚Ä¶,xQx_1,\ldots, x_Qx1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,xQ‚Äč, where xix_ixi‚Äč is the parameter of the iii-th query.
###Output
After each query, output in a single line the the sum of all elements of the current array modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

###Constraints


1‚Č§N‚Č§105 1\leq N\leq 10^51‚Č§N‚Č§105
1‚Č§Q‚Č§105 1\leq Q\leq 10^51‚Č§Q‚Č§105
‚ąí109‚Č§Ai‚Č§109 -10^9\leq A_i\leq 10^9‚ąí109‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§109
‚ąí105‚Č§xi‚Č§105 -10^5 \leq x_i\leq 10^5‚ąí105‚Č§xi‚Äč‚Č§105
